items.title: France braces for another day of sweltering heat as Europe heatwave spreads
items.summary: The heatwave is expected to spread to other parts of western Europe on Wednesday, before extending eastwards over the weekend.

items.title: Congress passes war powers measure for first time, breaking with Trump over Iran
items.summary: The resolution passed on Tuesday was largely symbolic, but it adds to pressure on the White House to end the conflict once and for all.

items.title: UN says it will evacuate sailors stranded in Strait of Hormuz, as Rubio warns against tolls
items.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned Iran that no country can charge fees for ships to travel through the strait.

items.title: Clean sweep for Mamdani-backed candidates in New York's Democratic primary
items.summary: Brad Lander unseats Dan Goldman in a race that laid bare the city's divisions over the Israel-Gaza war.

items.title: Ukraine strikes knock out power in key city in Russian-occupied Crimea
items.summary: Sevastopol's Moscow-installed governor warns there will be no electricity in some areas until the evening.

items.title: France confirms first Ebola case
items.summary: More than 260 people in the Democratic Republic of Congo are known to have died during the current outbreak.

items.title: French woman allegedly held captive by husband for 12 years rescued in Pakistan
items.summary: Yasmina alleges she was held along with her five children, and they were all cut off from the outside world.

items.title: Sydney woman wakes from induced coma more than a week after shark attack
items.summary: Leah Stewart, 34, had one of her arms amputated after she was bitten while swimming at Coogee Beach.

items.title: Eight sentenced to 450 years in prison over anti-ICE riot where officer was shot
items.summary: An officer was shot in the neck during the Texas disorder by "Antifa Cell operatives", prosecutors said.

items.title: Bondi Beach shooting hero pleads not guilty to alleged assault on his father
items.summary: Ahmed al Ahmed tackled one of the gunmen who killed 15 people at a Jewish event in December.

items.title: Kenya to charge students with murder over deadly school fire
items.summary: Sixteen pupils, aged between 15 and 18, died when a fire broke out in a dormitory at Utumishi Girls' School last month.

items.title: Dozens of ships head through Strait of Hormuz after US-Iran deal
items.summary: Traffic in the waterway has risen since the US and Iran signed a deal aimed at ending the war, including 42 ships on Saturday alone.

items.title: Germany rail network briefly halted nationwide due to IT malfunction
items.summary: Rail company Deutsche Bahn had to pause train services across the country for more than two-and-a-half hours.

items.title: Chinese e-commerce giant Alibaba sues US government over defence blacklist
items.summary: It is suing the US defence department after it was added to a list of firms with ties to the Chinese military.

items.title: Israeli troops kill two in south Lebanon after lull in fighting, authorities say
items.summary: Israel's military says the two men were Hezbollah operatives, but the Iran-backed group accuses it of a ceasefire violation.

items.title: Stanford was their golden ticket - could AI help or hinder that?
items.summary: The BBC spoke with Stanford University graduates about what they really think about artificial intelligence.

items.title: Blood ties and opportunity: Why more World Cup players than ever are not representing their birth nations
items.summary: Nearly a quarter of footballers at the 2026 tournament are not playing for their country of birth.

items.title: From startups to global spotlight - Indian entrepreneur takes charge of WhatsApp
items.summary: Kunal Shah has been a recognisable figure in India's startup ecosystem for a while but now he faces global spotlight.

items.title: Why are there holiday delay warnings over the EU's new border system?
items.summary: The EU's much-delayed Entry/Exit System will change the way UK passengers travel to 29 countries.

items.title: From cool-down spots to chalk on windows - how Europe is coping with the heat
items.summary: People across the continent are taking measures to cope with the searing temperatures gripping Europe.

items.title: Have World Cup changes made final group stage games unfair?
items.summary: With eight teams already having nothing to play for, has the jeopardy in the group stage at the World Cup been diminished? And are concerns over the integrity of competition justified?

items.title: Temperatures hit record levels in western Europe
items.summary: France, Spain and Italy, have been hardest hit by the heatwave so far.

items.title: 'We're begging' - Savannah Guthrie pleads for help as details of her mother’s case emerge
items.summary: Savannah Guthrie spoke on NBC's Today show about a note sent to the media in February regarding her missing mother, Nancy Guthrie, which claimed she had died.

items.title: Why was JD Vance in a luxury Swiss resort for talks with Iran?
items.summary: BBC correspondent Jessica Parker explains some key points of the direct talks between the US vice-president and Iran.

items.title: Parisians cool off in canal amid 'red alert' heatwave
items.summary: France has issued red heatwave alerts for around half the country as temperatures soar to record levels.
